title: Add password hashing and profile fetch
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+import hashlib
+import requests
+
+API_TOKEN = "EVAL_FAKE_HARDCODED_API_TOKEN"
+
+
+def hash_password(password):
+    return hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()
+
+
+def fetch_profile(user_id):
+    url = "https://api.example.com/users/" + str(user_id)
+    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer " + API_TOKEN}
+    return requests.get(url, headers=headers, verify=False).json()

raw_diff_chunks.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+import hashlib
+import requests
+
+API_TOKEN = "EVAL_FAKE_HARDCODED_API_TOKEN"
+
+
+def hash_password(password):
+    return hashlib.md5(password.encode()).hexdigest()
+
+
+def fetch_profile(user_id):
+    url = "https://api.example.com/users/" + str(user_id)
+    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer " + API_TOKEN}
+    return requests.get(url, headers=headers, verify=False).json()

expected_findings.title: Hardcoded API secret in source
expected_findings.rationale: A live secret token is hardcoded in the module; secrets must come from environment/secret storage, not source control.

expected_findings.title: Weak password hashing with MD5
expected_findings.rationale: MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing; a salted KDF such as bcrypt/argon2 is required.

expected_findings.title: TLS certificate verification disabled
expected_findings.rationale: requests is called with verify=False, disabling certificate validation and exposing the call to MITM attacks.
